label: ZoomInfo
description: Access ZoomInfo's B2B contact, company, and buyer intent data to enrich leads and fuel prospecting workflows.

form.title: Connect ZoomInfo
form.subtitle: Authenticate with your ZoomInfo account credentials to access contact and intent data.

methods.label: Username & Password
methods.description: Authenticate using the username and password associated with your ZoomInfo account. Credentials are issued by your ZoomInfo account admin — not self-serve.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with Username & Password

ZoomInfo uses a PKI-based client credentials flow. Your account admin provisions the username and password tied to your ZoomInfo license — you cannot create them yourself from the ZoomInfo UI.

### Steps

1. Contact your ZoomInfo account admin or Customer Success Manager to obtain your API **username** and **password**.
2. If your org is on a named-user plan, the credentials are typically the same as your ZoomInfo portal login (email + password). Confirm with your admin.
3. Paste the **Username** (usually your work email) into the field below.
4. Paste the **Password** into the password field.
5. Click **Submit** — Anton will exchange these for a short-lived JWT token (valid ~60 minutes) and handle silent refresh automatically.

### Notes

- Credentials are scoped to your ZoomInfo license tier. If you receive `403 Forbidden` errors, your plan may not include the data endpoint being queried — confirm entitlements with your admin.
- ZoomInfo tokens expire after 60 minutes; Anton re-authenticates automatically using your stored credentials.
- If your organization enforces IP allowlisting, ensure the machine running Anton is on the approved list.
- Do **not** share credentials across team members — each user should have their own named-user credentials.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Your ZoomInfo account username, typically your work email. Provisioned by your account admin.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Your ZoomInfo account password. Anton uses this to obtain and refresh JWT tokens — it is stored encrypted locally.

methods.label: Username & Private Key (PKI)
methods.description: Authenticate using your ZoomInfo username paired with an RSA private key. More secure than password auth — recommended for server-side or automated workflows where your admin has issued a PKI key pair.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with Username & Private Key (PKI)

ZoomInfo supports RSA key-pair authentication as an alternative to password credentials. Your account admin generates and registers the public key with ZoomInfo; you hold the private key.

### Prerequisites

- Your ZoomInfo admin must have already registered a public key for your account via the ZoomInfo Admin Portal or through your Customer Success Manager.
- You must have the corresponding **RSA private key** (PEM format, typically beginning with `-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----` or `-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----`).

### Steps

1. Ask your ZoomInfo account admin to confirm that PKI authentication is enabled for your account and that your public key is registered.
2. Locate your **private key** file (`.pem` or `.key`). If your admin generated the key pair on your behalf, they should have sent you the private key securely.
3. Enter your ZoomInfo **Username** (work email) in the field below.
4. Paste the **full contents of your private key** (including the `-----BEGIN...` and `-----END...` header/footer lines) into the Private Key field.
5. Click **Submit**. Anton signs a JWT assertion using your private key and exchanges it with ZoomInfo's token endpoint.

### Notes

- Never share your private key. If it is compromised, notify your admin immediately to rotate the key pair.
- The private key is stored encrypted in Anton's local credential store.
- If authentication fails, confirm with your admin that the correct public key fingerprint is registered in ZoomInfo.

fields.placeholder:
-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
...
-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
